key_insight: Compositional internal structure does not appear broadly under gradient descent; it emerges only in a narrow, target-dependent depth-and-connectivity regime, otherwise yielding fractured solutions.
engineering_relevance: For fracture and damage modeling, the result suggests that architecture choice—not just sparsity level—can determine whether a network learns reusable physical primitives or brittle, noncompositional representations.
method: Similarity-based pruning (SP) to recover compositional connectivity patterns
Heuristic depth predictor to estimate depths where compositionality is likely to emerge
Theoretical analysis using compositional sparsity, volume-ratio arguments, and feature-interference bounds
evidence_signal: The abstract reports empirical evaluation across varying sparsity/connectivity and depth, showing compositionality peaks only in specific sparse networks and at specific target-dependent depths.
watch_item: Similarity-based pruning and depth prediction appear regime-sensitive, so the method may require careful tuning and may not transfer across tasks or architectures without validation.
paper_title: Compositionality Emerges in a Narrow Depth-Connectivity Regime: Architecture Constraints and Solution Manifolds
